The answer is $200 - [cost of brushes] - [cost of canvas] - [cost of paint] = [net profit]. 

To calculate the net profit, we first determine the cost of each item:
- The brushes cost $20.
- The canvas costs three times more than the brushes, so it costs $20 * 3 = $60.
- The paint costs $8 per liter and she needs at least 5 liters, so it costs $8 * 5 = $40.

Now, subtract the total cost from the selling price:
$200 - $20 - $60 - $40 = $40.

So, Maria will earn [40] dollars on the painting.
